tokenization.whatIsTokenDesc: Tokens can be full words, subwords, or even punctuation. For example:
tokenization.vocabularyNote: This vocabulary is learned before training and reused for all inputs.
tokenization.whyMattersDesc: Tokens are the bridge between raw text and math — they allow language to be converted into vectors that neural networks can process.
tokenization.nextStepDesc: Each token will be mapped to an ID, which is then used to retrieve its embedding vector.
tokenization.title: Tokenization
tokenization.titleDesc: Text is broken into smaller units called tokens that the model can process.

tokenID.description: EACH TOKEN IS GIVEN A UNIQUE ID THAT CORRESPONDS TO A ROW IN THE EMBEDDING MATRIX
tokenID.matrixLabel: These IDs index into the embedding matrix
tokenID.matrixSlice: Embedding Matrix (~{vocabSize} × {dModel}) — showing a tiny slice
tokenID.title: Token IDs → Embeddings
tokenID.titleDesc: Each token is mapped to a unique integer ID, which selects a row in the embedding matrix.
tokenID.matrixInfoDesc: {modelName} stores all token embeddings in a large matrix of size:
tokenID.semanticsDesc: Tokens with similar meaning or usage are placed close together in this high-dimensional space.
tokenID.keyInsightDesc: Token IDs themselves have no meaning — they are just pointers into a learned vector space.

embedding.instruction: CLICK A TOKEN TO INSPECT ITS EMBEDDING VECTOR
embedding.title: Token Embeddings
embedding.titleDesc: Each token is converted into a high-dimensional vector representing meaning and context.
embedding.howItWorksDesc: Each token ID is mapped to a learned vector from an embedding table.
embedding.positionalEncodingDesc: The model adds positional information so it understands token order in the sequence.
embedding.finalEmbeddingDesc: Token embedding + positional encoding → final vector used by the transformer.

qkv.title: Query, Key & Value
qkv.titleDesc: Each token's embedding is linearly projected into three separate vectors — Q, K, and V — by using learned weight matrices.
qkv.queryDesc: What this token is looking for — like the search text you type into a search engine
qkv.keyDesc: What each token offers — like the title of a search result page that gets matched against the query
qkv.valueDesc: The actual content — once Q and K are matched, V is the information that gets retrieved and passed forward
qkv.formulaNote: Each weight matrix is learned during training and is different per layer — earlier layers capture syntax, deeper layers capture semantics.
qkv.multiHeadDesc: After computing Q, K, and V, each vector is split into {nHeads} heads. Each head independently attends to a different slice of the embedding.
qkv.attentionHeadsNote: Each head sees {floor} of the {dModel} embedding dims. Parallel specialisation gives the model richer representational power than a single head could.

selfAttention.instruction: CLICK A TOKEN TO SEE WHICH OTHER TOKENS IT PAYS ATTENTION TO
selfAttention.head: Head {head} / {nHeads}
selfAttention.error: Failed to fetch attention data. Is the backend running?
selfAttention.title: Masked Self-Attention
selfAttention.titleDesc: Each token computes how much it should attend to every other token, but only to tokens that came before it.
selfAttention.step1: Dot product of Q and K matrices produces a square score matrix
selfAttention.step2: Scores are scaled by √dₖ, then upper triangle is masked to −∞
selfAttention.step3: Softmax converts each row into probabilities summing to 1
selfAttention.step4: Dropout optionally zeros some weights during training
selfAttention.whyMaskDesc: {modelName} is a causal language model — the mask sets future positions to −∞ before softmax, collapsing them to 0% attention.
selfAttention.matrixDesc: Full attention matrix — row = query token, col = key token (Head {head})

attentionOut.animExplanation1: The attention matrix is (tokens x tokens) and each row sums to 1. The value matrix is (tokens x dims). This multiplication is valid because the shared inner dimension, number of tokens, matches on both sides.
attentionOut.animExplanation2: Each output row is a weighted average of all value rows. The attention weights act as mixing coefficients. Tokens with higher scores contribute more to the final result.
attentionOut.title: Output & Concatenation
attentionOut.titleDesc: Attention scores are used to compute a weighted sum of the Value vectors, producing the final output of the self-attention mechanism.
attentionOut.step1: Masked attention scores (lower triangle only) are multiplied with the Value matrix V
attentionOut.step2: Each row of V is scaled by its attention weight and summed — tokens attended to more contribute more
attentionOut.step3: The result is a new {floor}-dim vector per token, enriched with context from earlier tokens
attentionOut.multiHeadDesc: {modelName} runs {nHeads} attention heads in parallel. Currently showing head {head}.
attentionOut.concatenationDesc: The {nHeads} head outputs are concatenated then projected back down through a linear layer which merges all perspectives into one unified representation.
attentionOut.attentionHeadsNote: Each head sees the full sequence but attends to different parts.

mlp.title: MLP + Residual
mlp.titleDesc: Each token's attention output passes through a two-layer MLP that learns complex non-linear patterns.
mlp.step2: Linear layer (W₁) expands {floor} → {dModel} dims, applying learned weights + bias
mlp.step3: GELU activation — lets small values pass partially, large values fully
mlp.dimInspectorHint: hover any cell or type a dim (0–{dModelMinus}) to inspect
mlp.highDimNote: dims {floor}–{dModelMinus} only exist after the linear expansion — final vector only

output.step1Desc: The full representation of this token after all attention and MLP layers.
output.step2Desc: A learned weight matrix maps the hidden state to one raw score (logit) per vocabulary token.
output.step3Desc: Exponentiates every logit and normalises so all {vocabSize} values sum to exactly 1.
output.title: Linear + Softmax
output.titleDesc: After all transformer blocks, the final hidden state is projected into the full vocabulary space and converted into a probability distribution.
output.bullet1: Final vector enters from the last MLP block ({dModel} dims)
output.bullet2: Linear layer maps {dModel} → {vocabSize} dims, one raw logit per vocab token
output.bullet3: Larger logit = model thinks that token is more likely next
output.bullet4: Softmax normalises all logits into probabilities that sum to 1
output.whySoftmaxDesc: Logits can be any real number, negative or very large. The softmax exponential makes every value positive, and dividing by the sum forces all probabilities to add up to exactly 1.
output.vocabNote: Every token in the vocabulary gets a probability. Most collapse to near zero and only a handful compete.
output.predDesc1: The model uses the final representation of the last token to predict what word comes next.
output.predDesc2: Click any word to see how the sentence would continue with that prediction.
